summary.en: The city's hub for all things eclectic and artistic, where historic theaters, punk rock dive bars, and artist-run galleries coexist with a rebellious spirit.
Indianapolis's premier arts and culture corridor, a polished diagonal street where historic architecture houses contemporary theaters, artisan boutiques, and inventive cocktail bars.
A living museum of Victorian architecture, these grand residential streets feature ornate mansions and a compelling blend of old-world charm and new independent cafes.
A laid-back, leafy neighborhood with a distinct 'village within a city' feel, centered around the Central Canal and Monon Trail and known for its brewpubs and indie shops.
A compact, historic neighborhood that packs a culinary punch, this former Italian settlement has become a destination for food lovers with acclaimed eateries and distilleries.
The historic commercial heart of Indianapolis, where grand 19th-century buildings stand beside modern skyscrapers, and government, sports, and food halls converge.

description.en: A haven for retro gaming fans, this bar offers dozens of vintage arcade cabinets set to free-play, paired with local and regional craft beer.
An intimate and beloved music venue that's a perfect spot to grab a drink and see the next big thing in a listening room for true fans.
Located atop the historic Fountain Square Theatre Building, this seasonal spot offers fantastic views of the downtown skyline for a sunset drink.
Named for a Kurt Vonnegut novel, this literary-themed restaurant is a cornerstone of the local food scene with an inventive, seasonal menu.
A true hidden gem located inside a gas station, this counter-service spot serves surprisingly authentic and delicious tacos and burritos.
Expect the unexpected at this quirky venue, where programming ranges from burlesque shows with live bands to 'drunk spelling bees'.
Descend into this cozy, brick-walled basement bar for some of the city's best craft cocktails in an industrial-chic setting.
A slice of German history in the Athenæum building, this pre-WWII-style beer hall serves massive steins of German beer.
Housed in a restored 19th-century horse stable, Livery serves creative, Latin-inspired plates and features one of the best two-story patios in the city.
A long-standing neighborhood anchor, this sleek, modern spot is a local favorite for sushi and martinis with a lively atmosphere.
A towering, 38-foot mural of the city's most famous literary son gazes down on the avenue, an essential Indy landmark.
A beautifully curated shop dedicated to items made by over 300 Indiana artists and artisans, from jewelry to locally made jams.
This segment of the Cultural Trail features a dozen sculptural gardens dedicated to luminaries, offering serene moments of reflection.
An iconic electronic art piece on the corner of Mass Ave and Alabama St. that depicts a figure in perpetual, hypnotic motion.
A minimalist and modern coffee shop with a focus on quality, favored by locals for its serene atmosphere and excellent espresso.
A quintessential neighborhood dive bar in a historic corner building, offering a cozy, no-frills spot for a local beer.
An intimate, upscale-casual restaurant with a fantastic wine list and creative cocktails in a lively but refined setting.
A beloved gourmet market and deli that is an Indianapolis institution, famous for its legendary sandwiches and artisanal cheeses.
A vibrant and popular spot for modern Mexican cuisine and cocktails, with colorful decor and an energetic atmosphere.
A quirky and unique brunch spot with a subtle shoe theme that has quickly become a local favorite for its creative takes on breakfast classics.
A powerful sculpture commemorating the spot where Robert F. Kennedy announced MLK Jr.'s assassination, a profoundly moving and historic site.
The impeccably preserved home of the 23rd U.S. President, offering a fascinating, intimate look into Gilded Age life and politics.
Housed in a beautiful historic church, this non-profit arts organization features multiple galleries and dozens of artist studios.
Wander down Delaware or Alabama Street to admire the stunning, restored 19th-century mansions with ornate Italianate and Queen Anne designs.
One of the city's original craft breweries, this beloved local institution has the cozy, welcoming feel of an English pub right off the Monon Trail.
A legendary, no-frills dive bar that's a rite of passage for many locals, perfect for a cheap beer and a game of pool.
A long-standing independent coffee shop with a relaxed, bohemian vibe, great for grabbing a coffee before or after a walk on the Monon Trail.
A bright, farm-to-table cafe where 100% of profits go to a foundation that feeds food-insecure children in Indianapolis.
A Chicago-themed deli in SoBro that locals swear by, famous for its Italian beef sandwiches and smoked wings.
An intimate and authentic Neapolitan pizzeria where a wood-fired oven churns out perfectly blistered pies in a cozy, welcoming space.
A SoBro landmark for music lovers where you can flip through new and used vinyl and catch intimate, free in-store performances.
Situated on the banks of the White River, this center features galleries, an outdoor sculpture garden, and art classes.
For a quieter, more scenic walk, follow the gravel path along the historic Central Canal, which runs parallel to the White River.
A veteran-owned and operated distillery with a spacious, industrial-chic tasting room for sampling creative cocktails or tasting flights.
A craft distillery offering a fantastic tasting room experience with its own unique portfolio of spirits, including whiskeys, gins, and rums.
A nationally acclaimed, chef-driven brunch spot that has put Indianapolis on the culinary map with its constantly changing, innovative menu.
A hidden gem serving incredible, authentic tamales and other Mexican specialties that is a local favorite for a quick, delicious meal.
This world-class urban path runs directly through the neighborhood, connecting it seamlessly to downtown and Fountain Square.
The beautiful historic church that gives the neighborhood its name was the heart of the city's Italian community and remains an architectural landmark.
The massive and architecturally significant corporate campus of the pharmaceutical giant borders the neighborhood, a major part of the city's history and skyline.
Located on the second floor of the City Market, this bar is a sanctuary for local beer lovers, serving only Indiana-brewed craft beer.
A sleek, independent coffee shop just a block from Monument Circle, and a local favorite for a high-quality espresso drink.
A design-forward cocktail lounge known for its artistic cocktails and a stunning installation of 99 custom-blown glass lamps.
An Indianapolis institution since 1905, this classic Jewish deli serves legendary corned beef and pastrami sandwiches.
A modern food hall in a former Coca-Cola bottling plant offering a huge variety of local food and drink vendors.

name.en: Colonel Eli Lilly Civil War Museum

description.en: A true hidden gem located in the basement of the Soldiers' and Sailors' Monument, offering a look at Indiana's role in the Civil War.
The centerpiece of a massive 24-acre memorial district, this awe-inspiring neoclassical building houses a vast museum of U.S. military history.
Discover the brick-and-limestone tunnels from a demolished 19th-century building that lie hidden beneath the City Market.

prompt.en: Capture the glow of a neon sign reflecting on a wet pavement or window.
Find a piece of street art and take a selfie imitating the pose or mood of a character in it.
Take a photo that visually represents the music or energy you feel in the neighborhood right now.
Find the most unusual or eclectic decoration on a building's exterior and snap a close-up.
Photograph something that feels independent or goes against the grain.
Take a perspective shot looking down an alleyway, focusing on the art and textures.
Capture a detail that perfectly sums up the vibe of a local dive bar or music venue entrance.
Find a historic theater or entertainment building and photograph its most interesting architectural feature.
Take a selfie with a local craft beer or a plate of hot chicken.
Find the most vibrant splash of color in the neighborhood and make it the focus of your photo.
Take a close-up photo of a painted brick wall, a worn sticker, or another interesting urban texture.
After dark, capture the blur of passing car lights with the neighborhood's signs in the background.
Take a photo from the Cultural Trail that shows the flow of people, bikes, and city life.
Find a building that blends historic and modern design and capture the point where they meet.
Photograph a window display that you find particularly creative or stylish.
Find a piece of public art and take a photo of it from a very low or very high angle.
Capture the reflection of a historic building in the window of a modern shop or restaurant.
Take an artful photo of a craft cocktail or a fancy coffee drink.
Take a photo looking down the diagonal of Massachusetts Avenue, emphasizing its unique angle in the city grid.
Take a photo that you think Kurt Vonnegut would have appreciated—something ironic, human, or beautifully strange.
Capture the lively atmosphere of a sidewalk patio.
Find an object in a shop window that is clearly handmade and take a photo celebrating its craftsmanship.
Find an architectural detail or sign that hints at the neighborhood's German heritage.
Take a selfie that captures the polished, artistic vibe of the neighborhood.
Find an interesting architectural detail like 'gingerbread' trim, a stained-glass window, or an ornate door knocker and take a close-up shot.
Take a selfie in front of the grandest Victorian home you can find.
Capture the long shadows cast by the historic homes or old trees late in the afternoon.
Find a weathered texture—like old brick, peeling paint on a porch, or a rusty iron fence—and make it the subject of your photo.
Photograph the most impressive doorway or front porch you can find.
Capture the peaceful, residential feel of the neighborhood in a single photo.
Find a view that includes a historic home and a modern element, like a new car or a contemporary cafe.
Find a historic plaque or marker and take a thoughtful selfie next to it.
Take a photo of an artisanal food item, like a fancy cheese or a legendary sandwich.
Take a photo looking up at a castle-like turret or tower on a Queen Anne style house.
Find a home with a unique or beautiful color scheme and capture it.
Find a quiet park or memorial space and take a photo that conveys a sense of peace and reflection.
Take a photo of the sky, trees, or buildings reflected in the Central Canal.
Capture the movement on the Monon Trail. Try to get a sense of motion with walkers, runners, or cyclists.
Find a spot where nature and the urban environment meet and capture it in a single frame.
Take a photo that captures the relaxed, social atmosphere of a local pub or cafe patio.
Find a charming storefront or street corner that captures the 'village within a city' feel.
Take a selfie holding up a record album with interesting cover art.
Stand on a bridge over the canal or river and take a photo looking down the waterway.
Capture a shot of a tree-lined street that shows off the neighborhood's green canopy.
Take a photo of a delicious-looking farm-to-table dish.
Find a piece of outdoor sculpture or public art and photograph it with a natural background.
Find a detail—like a wind chime, a colorful sign, or a quirky garden—that shows off the neighborhood's bohemian side.
Take a picture of a pint from a local brewpub, with the pub's atmosphere in the background.
Take a top-down 'flat lay' photo of your coffee, pastry, or meal, arranging it artfully.
Find a sign or logo for a local distillery, bakery, or cafe and take a creative photo of it.
Take a photo focusing on the texture and pattern of the old brick buildings common in this area.
Capture a moment that shows this neighborhood's community feel—people chatting at a coffee shop, a busy counter, etc.
Take a mouth-watering photo of a fresh loaf of bread or a pastry in a bakery window.
Create a still life photo of a cocktail or a spirit tasting flight.
Find an architectural detail on a church or old building that hints at the neighborhood's Italian immigrant history.
Take a selfie that captures the excitement of waiting for a table at a popular brunch spot.
Photograph a design element that represents the 'industrial-chic' style, like exposed brick, metal fixtures, or reclaimed wood.
Take a photo of a place that looks unassuming from the outside but feels like a special local secret.
Take a photo that captures the warm, welcoming atmosphere of a community-focused coffee shop.
Find the most colorful dish or drink you can and make it the star of your photo.
Take a photo of one of the grand monuments or memorials from a low angle to emphasize its size and grandeur.
Frame a shot that includes both a historic 19th-century building and a modern skyscraper in the same view.
Find a high vantage point and take a photo looking down a street to show the city's 'spokes of a wheel' layout.
Take a colorful photo of the food on display at a market vendor or food hall.
Capture a detail on a historic commercial building—an old sign, a cornerstone, or ornate carving.
Take a photo of a classic, towering deli sandwich.
Find a doorway or gate that looks like it leads to a hidden or secret place and take a mysterious photo.
Take a photo that shows the mix of business and leisure in the district, like people in suits near a food hall.
Use the symmetrical architecture of a grand memorial or government building as the backdrop for a selfie.
Capture the energy and variety of a busy food hall.
Frame a shot of the city skyline as seen from street level, looking up between the tall buildings.
Take a respectful photo inside or outside a war memorial that conveys a sense of history and remembrance.
